**Diagnosis:** Late blight of tomato (*Solanum lycosanum*)
**Cause:** Water mold (*Pseudomonas syringae*) that thrives in warm, wet conditions. Spore release during blight causes secondary infections.
**Symptoms:** Black, water-soaked lesions on leaves turning mushy; blight on stems; black scab on r

**Diagnosis:** Early Blight (also known as Rust on Tomato)
**Causative Agent:** Fungus (e.g., *Fulvia folvia* or *Puccinia species*) depending on host, thrives in wet conditions.
**Symptoms:** Small, circular brown/orange spots on upper leaves; may coalesce; fuzzy mold on lower leaves if damp.
**Aff

**Diagnosis:** Tomato Bacterial Spot (Bacterial Leaf Spot)
**Causative Agent:** *Bse* (Tomato Bacterial Spot Epidemic Bumper Strain), *Bse* (Tomato Leaf Spot Bacterium)
**Symptoms:** Small, circular leaf spots with dark borders and lighter centers; leaf tissue may become leathery or brittle; prematu

**Diagnosis:** Tomato Leaf Mold (caused by *Sphagmospores* spores)
**Symptoms:** Fine, web-like mold growth on the upper leaves, especially affected by low soil moisture and high humidity. Can cause leaf distortion or premature death.
**Cause:** Water mold (*Phacidium* spp.) thrives in cool, moist c

**Diagnosis:** Septoria Leaf Spot (caused by *Septoria capsanthinensis*)
**Symptoms:** Small, circular brown spots with black borders on leaves; leaf discoloration; premature leaf death.
**Cause:** Fungus *Septoria capsanthinensis*. Spread by water splashes on foliage.
**Treatment:** Fungicides like

**Diagnosis:** Healthy Tomato
**Description:** Leaves are a vibrant green, no spots or discoloration, stems are strong and upright. Fruit (if present) is firm and correctly colored.
**Urgency:** Low - continue monitoring.
**Treatment:** Maintain consistent watering and feeding. Monitor for early sig

**Diagnosis:** Late blight of potato (*Solanum tuberosum*)
**Causative Agent:** Water mold (*Pseudomonas syringae*) followed by fungal infection (*Phytophthora infestans*)
**Symptoms:** Water-soaked lesions on leaves/stems, rapidly turning into greasy patches; white mold on affected foliage; black l

**Diagnosis:** Early Blight (also known as Potato Rust)
**Causative Agent:** Fungus (e.g., *Fusarium solanaci*, *Puccinia subspatatii*)
**Symptoms:** Small, circular brown/purplish spots on upper leaves; fine rust-like lines on lower leaves; premature wilting of affected plants.
**Causes:** Fungus t

**Diagnosis:** Healthy Potato (Solanum tuberosum)
**Appearance:** Smooth, oval to slightly irregular periderm (skin), light tan to white color, no bumps or lesions on the skin. Tuber is firm and odoriferous.
**Cause:** N/A - Plant is thriving.
**Prevention:** Maintain proper soil pH (4-6), adequate

**Diagnosis:** Bacterial Pepper Spot (or Bacterial Leaf Spot)
**Cause:** Buruli Streptococci bacteria transmitted by aphids
**Symptoms:** Small, circular leaf spots with dark borders and affected tissue; bacterial ooze on lesions; leaf roll/distortion; secondary infection on stems.
**Treatment:** Ba

**Diagnosis:** Aphid infestation (Primary Pest)
**Affected Area:** All parts of the plant, especially new growth and tender areas.
**Symptoms:** Tiny, fuzzy or waxy insects clustered together. Sticky foliage (honeydew). Infected leaves curl, turn yellow, and drop.
**Management:**

**Diagnosis:** Apple Scab
**Cercodes:** Cercodes cylindricus, Cercodes cylindricus var. vulgaris
**Symptoms:** Cylindrical (matching apple scab) or irregular brown/black scabs on leaves; rough texture matching scab; may coalesce. On trunk/branches, symptoms are more circular and raised.
**Cause:** F

**Diagnosis:** Black Rot (also known as Apple Core Rot or Black Heart)
**Causative Agents:** Fungi - *Botryosphaeria psilimensis* and *Botryosphaeria chrysensis*
**Affected Parts:** Inside of apple cores, black sunken areas on skin (Black Heart), rotting core
**Symptoms:** Black/blackish-green sunke

**Diagnosis:** Cedar Rust (or Juniper Rust)
**Causative Agent:** *Pseudomonas syringae* pv. *pspores* (a type of bacterial rust)
**Symptoms on Leaves:** Bright rusty-orange to brown pustules, especially on the upper leaf surface. Can cause distortion or premature leaf drop.
**Preferred Conditions:**

**Diagnosis:** Healthy Apple
**Description:** Apples are generally vibrant, with no visible signs of disease (scabs, mold, etc.). Fruits are firm and have no blemishes.
**Treatment:** Ongoing preventative care including fungicide applications during susceptible periods.
**Urgency:** Low - maintain v

**Diagnosis:** Common Rust (Rust fungus - *Puccinia sorghi*)
**Affected Area:** Leaves (raised rusty-orange/brown pustules)
**Cause:** Fungus spread by wind currents; thrives in warm, moist conditions.
**Symptoms:** Circular rust-colored spots on leaves; reduced photosynthesis; leaf death.

**Diagnosis:** Northern Corn Leaf Blade Rust (Pyracanidia cristata)
**Affected Areas:** Upper surfaces of corn leaves show rusty-brown, irregular patches. Affected areas may merge into large rusty patches.
**Cause:** Pyracanidia cristata (Pyracanidia cristata), common in hedgerows/parks. Causes Pyra

**Diagnosis:** Black Rot of Grapes (Caused by *Botrytis cinerea*)
**Symptoms:** Black, rotting clusters on vines; "Buskiness" (dense foliage); black mold (*Botrytis*) on berries; bitter taste.
**Cause:** Grey Mold (*Botrytis cinerea*); thrives in wet conditions with poor air circulation.
**Treatment

**Diagnosis:** Healthy Grapevine (Vitis vinifera)
**Symptoms:** Deep green color, no signs of distortion or disease, vigorous growth, no associated pests.
**Cause:** Optimal growing conditions (water, nutrients), absence of pathogens or pests.

🍅 토마토 후추잎마름병 (Late Blight) 🚨 **긴급 진단!**

**🔬 원인:** 역병균 (Verticillium dahliae), 고온다습에 급발생
**🔬 증상:** 아래쪽 줄기가 갈변, 물갈퀴 모양 갈변 반점, 심하면 쓰러짐
**🔬 치료:** 살균제 (만코배통, 코치브롬), 환기 개선, 감염

🥔 감자 후추잎마름병 (Late Blight) 🚨 **긴급 진단!** 잎이 검게 변하고 줄기가 쓰러지는 치명병!

🔬 **원인:** 역병균 (Phytophthora infestans) - 고온다습 시 급발병!
🔬 **증상:** 잎/줄기 검은 병반, 물때처럼 보이는 반점, 심하면 쓰러짐
🔬 **처방:**

🌱 토마토 건강 진단: 매우 건강함! (Excellent!)
현재 상태: 활발한 생육, 정상 잎 색상, 열매 정상 발육
진단: 건강한 토마토 (Healthy Tomato)
처방: 꾸준한 물주기, 균형 잡힌 비료 공급, 병충해 예찰
⭐ 중요도: 낮음 (현재 상태로 매우 좋음!)

🍎 사과  scab (껍질병) 진단 및 처방

**🔍 진단:** Apple scab (껍질병)
**🔬 증상:** 신엽(새로 자라는 잎)의 갈색 또는 검은색 병반(반점), 거칠고 병든 껍질처럼 변하는 성숙엽, 수침(水浸)형 병반
**🔬 원인:** 진균류 fungus cerasus var. scabius
**🔬

**진단:** 벼 녹병 (Common Rust of Rice)
**원인균:** *Puccinia sativa*
**증상:** 벼 줄기와 마디에 주황색 또는 갈색 반점, 포자낭군(sporangia), 심하면 고사
**처방:** 살녹병제 (예: 프로피톤, 호르아)를 예방적으로 사용. 물 빠진 토양 재배.
